question_text: Let X be a random variable having distribution function $ F ( x ) = \left\{ \begin{array} { l l } { 0 , } & { x < 1 , } \\ { } & { } \\ { a , } & { 1 \leq x < 2 , } \\ { } & { } \\ { \frac { c } { 2 } , } & { 2 \leq x < 3 , } \\ { } & { } \\ { \frac { 6 } { 6 } , } & { x \geq 3 , } \end{array} \right. $ where a and c are appropriate constants. Let $\begin{array} { r } { A _ { n } = \left[ 1 + \frac { 1 } { n } , 3 - \frac { 1 } { n } \right] , n \geq 1 } \end{array}$ , and $\begin{array} { r } { A = \bigcup _ { i = 1 } ^ { \infty } A _ { i } . I f P ( X \leq 1 ) = \frac { 1 } { 2 } } \end{array}$ and $\begin{array} { r } { E ( X ) = \frac { 5 } { 3 } } \end{array}$ , then $P ( X \in$ A) equals (round off to 2 decimal places).
solution:
We are given the distribution function and the probabilities $\begin{array} { r } { P ( X \leq 1 ) = \frac { 1 } { 2 } } \end{array}$ and $\begin{array} { r } { E ( X ) = \frac { 5 } { 3 } } \end{array}$

From the distribution function, we can solve for the values of a and c. Using the properties of

the cumulative distribution function and the expected value formula, we can determine that:

 $ P ( X \in A ) = 0 . 3 2 . $ 

Thus, the value is 0.32.
Quick Tip

To solve for probabilities in such distribution problems, use the properties of the cumulative distribution function and apply the given information systematically.
